title: K0NSULT uni0nai DID-Agent
description: Soulbound identity document for a NON-HUMAN agent. Doctrine: claim<=proof, agents-not-people (zero person PII), soulbound != crypto (non-transferable), No Password Custody (public key only, tool never holds a private key). conformance.mjs enforces the KEY rules (R1..R6) marked below; this schema is the human-readable contract.
$comment_r6: R6 (enforced): the document is CLOSED. additionalProperties:false is the structural allowlist — an unenumerated person-PII key (full_name, phone, address, date_of_birth, ...) can no longer ride through as a clean agent. A denylist alone could never enumerate them all.

id.description: R7 (enforced by conformance.mjs DID_RE): id MUST match did:k0nsult:<provider>:<model>:<role>, e.g. did:k0nsult:anthropic:opus-4-8:judge. Pattern mirrors conformance.mjs so schema and validator do not drift.

subject_type.description: R1 (enforced): MUST equal the constant 'agent'. Any other value or absence => FAIL. Guarantees no natural person is the subject.

public_key.description: R5 (enforced): public key material only. Private-key fields (private_key, secret_key, seed, mnemonic, JWK 'd') anywhere in the document => FAIL. The tool never generates or holds a private key; signing happens with the operator, outside the tool.

skills.description: R3 (enforced): each skill MUST carry a non-empty evidence_class. A skill without evidence_class => FAIL (claim<=proof).

evidence_class.description: Evidence tier backing the skill claim (e.g. A/B/C).

token.description: R4 (enforced): soulbound token. non_transferable MUST be exactly true. additionalProperties:false closes the object so any transfer-shaped field (transfer, transferable, transfer_to, transfer_hook, approve, allowance, ...) at ANY depth => FAIL. conformance.mjs additionally scans transfer-shaped keys DEEP (nested transfer_to under token cannot slip past). Soulbound is an identity binding, NOT a crypto asset.

rotation_ref.description: Fingerprint (sha256:<hex>) of the immediately-prior PUBLIC key after a rotation (did-resolver.rotate). Public-key chain pointer only; carries no secret.

key_rotations.description: Append-only chain of PUBLIC-key rotation events (did-resolver.rotate). No private material, ever (No Password Custody).

$comment: R2 (enforced by conformance.mjs, not expressible cleanly per-property here): person PII keys (person, email/e-mail, pesel, national_id, ssn) are forbidden ANYWHERE in the document tree => FAIL. R6 (additionalProperties:false above) is the top-level structural allowlist.
